meta.name: Yellow Hollow
meta.tagline: A pristine suburb where the storm drains bleed ink and every favor comes with an interest rate.

aesthetic.visualStyle: Desaturated ochre and mint-green suburban geometry bleeding into warped, heavy-ink hellscapes; crisp 1990s consumerism juxtaposed with folk-horror textures like rusted rebar, dried corn husks, and flickering CRT static.
aesthetic.tone: Eerie yet wistful, punctuated by sudden drops into slapstick chaos or quiet cosmic dread, always anchored by warm, familiar lighting.
themes: Transactional relationships masked by neighborhood politeness
Childhood wonder curdling into bureaucratic horror
Loyalty as the only currency that doesn't depreciate
The weight of inherited and self-imposed obligations
inspirations: MAD Magazine's Treehouse of Horror segments
Edward Hopper meets Tim Burton with Kafkaesque paperwork

archetype:the-grounded-provider.name: The Grounded Provider
archetype:the-grounded-provider.tagline: Tactical parent who weaponizes domesticity and duty.
archetype:the-grounded-provider.description: When taking damage, the Provider may choose to take half damage but must spend their next turn recovering (standing still, breathing heavily). If they successfully protect another character from an attack using the Picket Fence Shield during this time, the recovery ends immediately.

features.name: Naptime Zone
features.description: When taking damage, the Provider may choose to take half damage but must spend their next turn recovering (standing still, breathing heavily). If they successfully protect another character from an attack using the Picket Fence Shield during this time, the recovery ends immediately.

archetype:the-grounded-provider.background: You once organized the neighborhood block party despite a hurricane warning. The HOA gave you a plaque; you framed it upside down. You have a mental spreadsheet of every debt owed by every family on the cul-de-sac, including interest calculated in favors.
archetype:the-grounded-provider.flavor: "I didn't survive the nineties to lose a daughter to a demon with a clipboard."

archetype:the-gag-legged-kid.name: The Gag-Legged Kid
archetype:the-gag-legged-kid.tagline: Survives through cartoon physics and chaotic improvisation.
archetype:the-gag-legged-kid.description: As a reaction to being attacked, the Kid can trigger a piece of cartoon logic (e.g., an anvil falls, a tunnel appears behind them). They take no damage from that specific attack, but the environment changes permanently (a crater forms, a hole is dug).

features.name: Anvil Drop
features.description: As a reaction to being attacked, the Kid can trigger a piece of cartoon logic (e.g., an anvil falls, a tunnel appears behind them). They take no damage from that specific attack, but the environment changes permanently (a crater forms, a hole is dug).

archetype:the-gag-legged-kid.background: You believe your stuffed animal is actually a guardian spirit, though it might just be glue and stuffing. You were the first one to notice the storm drains bubbling, but everyone thought you were just playing make-believe.
archetype:the-gag-legged-kid.flavor: "If I run fast enough, gravity forgets to look at me!"

archetype:the-ledger-keeper.name: The Ledger Keeper
archetype:the-ledger-keeper.tagline: Bureaucratic negotiator who treats contracts like spells.
archetype:the-ledger-keeper.description: The Keeper can force a creature to adhere to a minor rule or deadline for one round. If the creature violates the rule (e.g., "You cannot move until you answer a question"), they suffer disadvantage on their next action.

features.name: Red Tape Bind
features.description: The Keeper can force a creature to adhere to a minor rule or deadline for one round. If the creature violates the rule (e.g., "You cannot move until you answer a question"), they suffer disadvantage on their next action.

archetype:the-ledger-keeper.background: You used to work in claims adjustment for a mid-tier insurance firm before the sky turned yellow. You miss the paperwork. You know exactly which demon owes your family money from a deal made in 1998.
archetype:the-ledger-keeper.flavor: "Read the fine print. It’s where the teeth are."

archetype:the-block-captain.name: The Block Captain
archetype:the-block-captain.tagline: Guardian of hidden routes and unspoken hierarchies.
archetype:the-block-captain.description: The Captain can designate any two adjacent locations as connected for one turn, allowing characters to bypass obstacles or enemies by slipping through "neighborhood gaps" (fences, hedges, shadows).

features.name: Shortcuts & Alleys
features.description: The Captain can designate any two adjacent locations as connected for one turn, allowing characters to bypass obstacles or enemies by slipping through "neighborhood gaps" (fences, hedges, shadows).

archetype:the-block-captain.background: You’ve lived here since before the houses were built. You remember when the cul-de-sac was just a dirt circle and a well. You keep a key to every garage on the street, mostly because you never know when you’ll need to hide something.
archetype:the-block-captain.flavor: "I know where the bodies are buried because I helped dig the holes."

item:candy-bowl-of-holding.name: Candy Bowl of Holding
item:candy-bowl-of-holding.description: A ceramic bowl shaped like a grinning jack-o'-lantern, glazed in sickly yellow, with a chip in its lip that drips a viscous amber syrup. Can store up to 50 pounds of small objects within its interior volume regardless of external size. Once per long rest, the owner may consume a handful of the bowl's ambient candy to regain 1d8 hit points or remove one minor condition. If the bowl is emptied completely, it demands a secret truth from the owner to refill itself.
item:candy-bowl-of-holding.flavor: The bottom never touches the bottom, and the candy always tastes like whatever you promised yourself you'd eat later.

item:leaf-blower-of-gale-force.name: Leaf Blower of Gale Force
item:leaf-blower-of-gale-force.description: A rusted orange Toro model with a red pull-cord that screams in human agony when tugged, and a nozzle that winks at passersby. Ranged weapon dealing 1d8 force damage. On a hit, the target must succeed on a CON save or be knocked prone and blown back 10 feet. Can be used to clear obstacles, extinguish small fires, or blow away lightweight cover. Requires a pull-cord tug action to activate.
item:leaf-blower-of-gale-force.flavor: It doesn't just move leaves; it moves expectations, dignity, and occasionally the roof shingles of your worst enemies.

item:hoa-bylaw-tome.name: HOA Bylaw Tome
item:hoa-bylaw-tome.description: A leather-bound binder thick enough to stop a bullet, filled with photocopied regulations written in shifting ink that rearranges itself based on the reader's guilt. Grants advantage on checks to enforce neighborhood rules or cite violations. The bearer can invoke a Bylaw to impose a penalty on a target who lacks a proper permit or breaks a local custom, forcing a WIT save or suffering disadvantage on their next action. Requires reading aloud a relevant paragraph.
item:hoa-bylaw-tome.flavor: Section 4, Paragraph B: Thou shalt not howl after sunset without filing Form 99-Z in triplicate, or risk having your mail delivered to the abyss.

item:reflective-vest-of-invisibility.name: Reflective Vest of Invisibility
item:reflective-vest-of-invisibility.description: A neon-yellow safety vest with silver stripes that seem to drink the light around them, stitched with thread that hums when someone is lying. While worn, the character is invisible if they stand perfectly still and face a reflective surface. Additionally, grants resistance to effects that rely on sight, such as gaze attacks or charm spells targeting visual recognition. The vest clings uncomfortably to the skin.
item:reflective-vest-of-invisibility.flavor: You're only invisible if you're wearing this and nobody's looking directly at your face; peripheral vision is the enemy of the suburban ghost.

item:bag-of-trick-or-treats.name: Bag of Trick Or Treats
item:bag-of-trick-or-treats.description: A paper sack printed with a smiling ghost, heavy as lead and warm to the touch, with a drawstring that tightens when shaken. D20 roll upon reaching inside: 1-10 yields a treat (restore 1d6 HP or grant +1 to next roll); 11-15 yields a trick (disadvantage on next check or take 1d4 psychic damage); 16-19 summons a harmless but distracting minor spirit; 20 reveals a clue about a nearby danger or treasure.
item:bag-of-trick-or-treats.flavor: Always check the bottom before you reach in; the layer at the bottom is always deeper than the top.

consumable.effectDescription: D20 roll upon reaching inside: 1-10 yields a treat (restore 1d6 HP or grant +1 to next roll); 11-15 yields a trick (disadvantage on next check or take 1d4 psychic damage); 16-19 summons a harmless but distracting minor spirit; 20 reveals a clue about a nearby danger or treasure.

item:garden-hose-hydra.name: Garden Hose Hydra
item:garden-hose-hydra.description: A standard green garden hose that writhes when water pressure hits it, sprouting nozzle-heads that chatter like teeth and chase moving shadows. Melee weapon dealing 1d6 bludgeoning plus 1d6 water damage. On a hit, the hose can lash out again on the user's next turn as a bonus action without using an action, provided water pressure is maintained. Constricts grappled targets for 1d6 damage per round.
item:garden-hose-hydra.flavor: Turn the tap and watch the neighbors' petunias scream; this hose remembers every drought and holds a grudge.

item:remote-control-of-rewind.name: Remote Control of Rewind
item:remote-control-of-rewind.description: A chunky grey remote with a rubbery 'REW' button and a dial labeled 'PLOT', featuring a battery compartment that houses a tiny trapped moth. Once per day, pressing REW rewinds time by 6 seconds for the user and immediate surroundings, allowing a re-roll of a failed check or undoing a movement. The dial can be set to 'FAST' to grant haste-like speed for 10 seconds, but causes the user to age slightly visibly.
item:remote-control-of-rewind.flavor: Press play when you're ready to make the same mistake twice; the universe loves a rerun, but charges extra for the commercial break.

item:thermos-of-eternal-coffee.name: Thermos of Eternal Coffee
item:thermos-of-eternal-coffee.description: A dented stainless steel thermos stamped with 'WORLD'S OKAYEST DAD', never running cold or hot, smelling of burnt hazelnuts and unresolved trauma. Drinking a cup restores 1d4 stamina points or removes one level of exhaustion. As a reaction when reduced to 0 HP, the user can take a sip to drop to 1 HP instead and gain temporary immunity to fear effects for one minute. The coffee improves with every sip, becoming more potent.
item:thermos-of-eternal-coffee.flavor: It keeps you awake through the apocalypse, the divorce, and the third act twist, because someone has to drive the minivan.

consumable.effectDescription: Drinking a cup restores 1d4 stamina points or removes one level of exhaustion. As a reaction when reduced to 0 HP, the user can take a sip to drop to 1 HP instead and gain temporary immunity to fear effects for one minute. The coffee improves with every sip, becoming more potent.

item:detention-slip-of-binding.name: Detention Slip of Binding
item:detention-slip-of-binding.description: A crisp white slip of paper with red margins, smelling of chalk dust and sulfur, bearing a signature that shifts between familiar faces. When pinned to a creature, binds them to a specific location or task until a valid authority figure signs the slip. The target cannot leave the area or complete the task willingly. Breaking the slip requires a successful WIT check or destroying the pin with a mundane object.
item:detention-slip-of-binding.flavor: Detention lasts forever if the teacher forgets to clock out; the hallway stretches endlessly behind closed doors.

item:walkie-talkie-of-whispers.name: Walkie-Talkie of Whispers
item:walkie-talkie-of-whispers.description: A pair of clip-on radios with tangled antennas that twitch toward secrets, emitting a static that sounds like voices arguing in the other room. Allows the user to hear whispers from up to a mile away if concentrating for one minute. Can also transmit voice over short distances, bypassing normal line-of-sight. If both units are held by different people, they share a empathic link, sensing each other's emotional state.
item:walkie-talkie-of-whispers.flavor: Static is just the dead trying to complain about the noise; tune carefully and you might hear your mother calling from the basement.

item:pressure-washer-of-purification.name: Pressure Washer of Purification
item:pressure-washer-of-purification.description: A high-pressure wand mounted on a wheeled tank, hissing steam that smells of lemon pledge and brimstone, with a nozzle that spins like a drill. Ranged weapon dealing 1d8 force damage. On a hit, washes away one condition affecting the target, such as dirt, blood, grease, or minor curses. High pressure can knock targets prone. Running out of soap reduces damage to 1d4 and adds a sticky coating that imposes disadvantage on Dex saves.
item:pressure-washer-of-purification.flavor: Get off my lawn! And get off my soul! The spray erases sins right along with the mildew.

item:picket-fence-shield.name: Picket Fence Shield
item:picket-fence-shield.description: A section of white wooden fence with pointed slats, reinforced with iron bands and painted with a smiley face that fades when threatened. Provides +2 AC while wielded. Can be thrown as a melee weapon dealing 1d6 slashing damage, then returns to the thrower's hand. If planted in the ground, creates a barrier that blocks movement and grants cover to allies behind it for one minute.
item:picket-fence-shield.flavor: Good fences make good neighbors; great fences keep the ghouls out and the HOA fines in.

item:jolly-rancher-of-fire-breath.name: Jolly Rancher of Fire Breath
item:jolly-rancher-of-fire-breath.description: A hard candy wrapped in crinkling foil, colored a violent, pulsating crimson, feeling warm like a living thing against the tongue. As a bonus action, the consumer exhales a 15-foot cone of fire dealing 2d6 fire damage. This effect lasts for one minute or until the candy dissolves (three uses). Afterward, the user's throat is raw, imposing disadvantage on the first verbal spell cast.
item:jolly-rancher-of-fire-breath.flavor: Watermelon flavor, infernal temperature; melts in your mouth, not in your hand, unless you're holding a demon.

consumable.effectDescription: As a bonus action, the consumer exhales a 15-foot cone of fire dealing 2d6 fire damage. This effect lasts for one minute or until the candy dissolves (three uses). Afterward, the user's throat is raw, imposing disadvantage on the first verbal spell cast.

item:trunk-of-backwards-gears.name: Trunk of Backwards Gears
item:trunk-of-backwards-gears.description: An antique steamer trunk covered in brass gears that spin counter-clockwise, ticking loudly and emitting a smell of ozone and old memories. Contains a mechanism that, when activated, reverses gravity or polarity in a 10-foot cube for one minute. Objects and creatures fall upward. Alternatively, can be opened to retrieve one item lost within the last hour, pulled out backwards through time.
item:trunk-of-backwards-gears.flavor: Time flows backward here, so don't forget to unpack yesterday; the contents arrive before you put them in.

item:measuring-tape-of-stretching.name: Measuring Tape of Stretching
item:measuring-tape-of-stretching.description: A yellow tape measure that extends infinitely and snaps back with bone-jarring force, marked in inches that sometimes spell out words. Can be used to grapple or trip targets within 30 feet by snapping the tape. Measures magical distances accurately. When retracted rapidly, can strike as a whip dealing 1d4 piercing damage. Extending the tape allows climbing or bridging gaps up to 60 feet.
item:measuring-tape-of-stretching.flavor: One inch for me, a thousand miles for thee; the numbers lie when you're afraid of the dark.

item:can-of-spray-paint-reality.name: Can of Spray Paint Reality
item:can-of-spray-paint-reality.description: A rattle-can of metallic gold paint labeled 'DO NOT SHAKE' with a skull logo, dripping a substance that shimmers between colors. Three charges. Spraying a surface creates a portal to a random nearby location, writes a command into reality that takes effect immediately, or alters properties of the object (e.g., painting a door makes it float). Shaking the can increases potency but risks exploding.
item:can-of-spray-paint-reality.flavor: Shake well before warping the fabric of existence; the label warns you, but you never listen until the wall starts breathing.

consumable.effectDescription: Three charges. Spraying a surface creates a portal to a random nearby location, writes a command into reality that takes effect immediately, or alters properties of the object (e.g., painting a door makes it float). Shaking the can increases potency but risks exploding.

item:garage-door-opener-key.name: Garage Door Opener Key
item:garage-door-opener-key.description: A plastic fob with a single red button that hums with low-frequency dread, connected by a frayed wire to a keychain of bones. Opens any garage door, even if there is no garage. Can open doors to other planes if the target looks like a garage or resembles one conceptually. Pressing the button emits a sound that disorients undead and spirits, granting them disadvantage on their next attack.
item:garage-door-opener-key.flavor: Bzzzt. Welcome to the basement of the world; the car is parked in the void, and the oil change is due in eternity.

item:filing-cabinet-of-souls.name: Filing Cabinet of Souls
item:filing-cabinet-of-souls.description: A metal cabinet with drawers that slide open and shut on their own, moaning softly, containing folders labeled with names that shift as you read them. Stores up to five souls or memories. A stored soul can be retrieved to speak briefly or possess a willing host for one scene. Filing a soul grants the owner insight into that being's secrets. Opening the wrong drawer releases a wisp that imposes disadvantage on the next WIT check.
item:filing-cabinet-of-souls.flavor: File under 'A' for Ambition, or 'D' for Damnation; the system works perfectly if you don't look too closely at the handwriting.

item:snowblower-of-stasis.name: Snowblower of Stasis
item:snowblower-of-stasis.description: A bright red snowblower with a chute that spews absolute zero instead of snow, vibrating with a sound that freezes tears on cheeks. Ranged weapon dealing 1d8 cold damage. On a hit, the target is encased in ice for one round, restrained and unable to take reactions. Area effect slows enemies in a 10-foot radius by half speed. Fuel consumption is measured in breaths taken by the operator.
item:snowblower-of-stasis.flavor: Clearing the path to oblivion, one drift at a time; the snow never melts, and neither do the memories buried beneath it.

item:mirror-of-self-reflection.name: Mirror of Self-Reflection
item:mirror-of-self-reflection.description: A hand mirror with a silver frame shaped like weeping willows, showing the viewer's true self rather than their appearance, eyes glowing faintly. Wearer gains advantage on perception checks to detect lies, illusions, or hidden weaknesses. As an action, the wearer can gaze into the mirror to reveal one secret about themselves or an ally, gaining temporary HP equal to their WIT modifier. Looking too long risks seeing a future regret.
item:mirror-of-self-reflection.flavor: What do you see? Not what you want to see; the glass knows you better than you know yourself, and it's judging.

monster:lawnmower-maw.name: Lawnmower Maw
monster:lawnmower-maw.description: A weed-whacker-headed beast with rotating blade jaws that chew through both foliage and fragile resolve.

attacks.name: Blade Snap
attacks.flavor: Three metal plates swing shut with a sound like snapping twigs, spraying wood chips and static charge.

attacks.name: Mulch Spit
attacks.flavor: A glob of acidic compost erupts from its spinning head, smelling of wet cardboard and burnt hair.

specialAbilities: Overheat: After landing two attacks, steam vents loudly from its neck joint. Grants advantage on its next attack but deals 1 point of self-damage as internal gears grind.

tactics.specialBehavior: Skitters quickly through brush, targets isolated players, uses terrain for cover. Prioritizes players carrying snacks or candy bowls.

monster:lawnmower-maw.firstAction: Revs its head and charges toward the nearest player holding a snack or candy, aiming to knock them into a mulch bed.
monster:lawnmower-maw.lore: Feeds on overgrowth and anxiety. Nests in mulch beds near storm drains. Avoids loud noises but loves the sound of a distant garage door opener. Leaves behind neatly chopped grass and scattered house keys.
monster:lawnmower-maw.encounterText: A familiar suburban rumble cuts through the damp air before the thing tears through a hydrangea bush. Its head is a rusted weed whacker spinning lazily; its jaw unhinges into three interlocking metal blades that click like castanets.

monster:hoarding-imp.name: Hoarding Imp
monster:hoarding-imp.description: A tiny, twitchy creature that clutches misplaced socks, lost remotes, and forgotten promises in a burlap sack.

attacks.name: Pocket Dump
attacks.flavor: Showering gravel, bottle caps, and a single earring that rings like a bell upon impact.

attacks.name: Snatch
attacks.flavor: Quick, needle-thin fingers hook onto a belt loop or pocket, yanking hard enough to dislodge gear.

specialAbilities: Bargain: Can trade a stolen item for safe passage if the party offers something equally mundane but emotionally weighted (e.g., a child's drawing, a wedding ring). Requires a WIT check to negotiate successfully.

tactics.specialBehavior: Harasses from range, tries to steal small items, uses hit-and-run tactics. Will flee if offered a meaningful trinket.

monster:hoarding-imp.firstAction: Darts behind a planter, pulls out a remote control, and points it expectantly at the party while clicking buttons rapidly.
monster:hoarding-imp.lore: Collects lost things and unkept promises. Trades them for minor favors. Sleeps curled inside hollow tree stumps or abandoned mailboxes. Terrified of being seen doing nothing.
monster:hoarding-imp.encounterText: It drops from a gutter spout with a clatter of plastic and fabric. No bigger than a garden gnome, it wears a visor made of stacked coasters and frantically pats down its oversized burlap sack, which bulges with mismatched mittens and crumpled receipt paper.

monster:asphalt-wraith.name: Asphalt Wraith
monster:asphalt-wraith.description: A shimmering heat-haze figure that drags victims into freshly poured road patches, leaving only tire tracks behind.

attacks.name: Tar Drag
attacks.flavor: Heavy suction sound as black goo wraps around ankles, pulling downward with the weight of a compact car.

attacks.name: Heat Haze Strike
attacks.flavor: Blinding shimmer followed by a solid, paving-stone impact that cracks the ground beneath the target.

specialAbilities: Fresh Pour: Creates a new patch of asphalt as a bonus action, turning adjacent terrain into difficult ground. Lasts until cooled (3 rounds) or exposed to extreme cold.

tactics.specialBehavior: Controls space, slows movement, targets players trying to advance or retreat. Uses newly created asphalt to separate the party.

monster:asphalt-wraith.firstAction: Slides forward, coating the ground in a slick layer of sticky bitumen that impedes footwork and creates difficult terrain.
monster:asphalt-wraith.lore: Born where suburban expansion meets old earth. Waits at crosswalks and freshly paved cul-de-sacs. Leaves only rubber marks and a faint ringing in the ears of those who escape. Moves slower in rain.
monster:asphalt-wraith.encounterText: The pavement ahead ripples like hot tar. A silhouette stretches upward, composed of cooling blacktop and crushed gravel, leaving deep tire treads in the air itself. It smells of ozone and wet chalk.

monster:inkblot-scribe.name: Inkblot Scribe
monster:inkblot-scribe.description: A floating quill-manipulator that writes binding clauses in midair, forcing readers to obey whatever it scrawls.

attacks.name: Clause Draft
attacks.flavor: Ink sprays in geometric patterns, staining clothes and skin with words that itch when read aloud.

attacks.name: Redaction Touch
attacks.flavor: A black smudge wipes across the target's mind or inventory, temporarily erasing a known skill or item.

specialAbilities: Fine Print: When it writes a clause, any creature reading it must make a WIT save or obey the instruction until the ink dries (1 minute). Breaking eye contact ends the effect.

tactics.specialBehavior: Ranged controller, forces compliance via written effects, avoids melee. Targets spellcasters and leaders first.

monster:inkblot-scribe.firstAction: Begins drafting a clause in the air, eyes locking onto the party leader while scratching a glowing signature into reality.
monster:inkblot-scribe.lore: Enforces neighborhood covenants and demon contracts alike. Lives in abandoned HOA offices or flooded filing rooms. Eats punctuation marks and regrets. Communicates exclusively through marginalia.
monster:inkblot-scribe.encounterText: It hovers at eye level, a floating cluster of ink-stained fingers and brass pen nibs. With a scratchy hiss, it drafts glowing paragraphs in the humid air, letters curling like smoke before snapping into sharp focus.

monster:tupperware-ghoul.name: Tupperware Ghoul
monster:tupperware-ghoul.description: A lumpy, lid-clattering horror that seals intruders in airtight containers, slowly suffocating them with stale air.

attacks.name: Lid Slam
attacks.flavor: Deafening plastic crack as a heavy lid drops, creating instant cover and pinning limbs beneath.

attacks.name: Seal Tight
attacks.flavor: Wraps target in cling-film-like energy, reducing mobility and muffling all verbal components.

specialAbilities: Airlock: Can trap a target in a container as a reaction. Target suffocates after 3 rounds unless broken free (CON save) or given a drinking straw.

tactics.specialBehavior: Grapples, traps, uses containers to block paths or isolate targets. Slow but highly durable in close quarters.

monster:tupperware-ghoul.firstAction: Unlatches its chest cavity, revealing a stack of empty, humming containers ready to snap shut over approaching feet.
monster:tupperware-ghoul.lore: Preserves things too perfectly. Steals leftovers and people alike, storing them in cool, dark places. Terrified of sudden temperature changes. Hoards expired coupons and half-used candles.
monster:tupperware-ghoul.encounterText: It lumbers forward, body composed of stacked, translucent plastic tubs with mismatched lids rattling like teeth. The center glows faintly orange, and the clatter grows louder with every step.

monster:cul-de-sac-cyclops.name: Cul-de-Sac Cyclops
monster:cul-de-sac-cyclops.description: A towering figure with a single surveillance camera for an eye, patrolling roundabouts and demanding tolls in memories.

attacks.name: Zoom Lens Blast
attacks.flavor: Concentrated light burns through retinas, leaving afterimages and temporary blindness.

attacks.name: Toll Collection
attacks.flavor: Backhand swipe that steals a skill or use for a scene, accompanied by the sound of a cash register closing.

specialAbilities: Pan & Tilt: As a reaction, can reposition itself up to 15 feet along the road, gaining advantage on attacks against creatures previously targeted this turn.

tactics.specialBehavior: Zone control, demands tribute, uses camera zoom/blind spots. Prioritizes breaking party formation and isolating scouts.

monster:cul-de-sac-cyclops.firstAction: Raises a clawed hand, camera lens focusing with a bright flash, scanning the party for valuable recollections.
monster:cul-de-sac-cyclops.lore: Guards the intersections between neighborhoods and the underworld. Demands tolls paid in nostalgia or childhood secrets. Stores collected memories in glass jars buried under streetlights. Hates being photographed.
monster:cul-de-sac-cyclops.encounterText: A monolith of concrete and chain-link rises from the asphalt circle. Its face is a rusted traffic camera housing, lens dilating with a mechanical whir. It doesn't walk so much as roll forward on hydraulic stilts, casting long, distorted shadows.

monster:paperclipped-serpent.name: Paperclipped Serpent
monster:paperclipped-serpent.description: A slithering coil of bent metal and shredded memo paper that binds targets and drags them toward filing drawers.

attacks.name: Bind & File
attacks.flavor: Coils tighten with metallic screeches, pinning arms and forcing the target toward a designated storage zone.

attacks.name: Paper Cut Barrage
attacks.flavor: Razor-sharp memo fragments spray outward, leaving precise, bleeding lines across clothing and skin.

specialAbilities: Filing Deadline: If left unaddressed for 2 rounds, it begins dragging all adjacent creatures toward the nearest large drawer or cabinet at half speed.

tactics.specialBehavior: Entangles, drags toward specific locations (filing cabinets/drawers), uses paper cuts/shredding. Focuses on immobilizing key targets.

monster:paperclipped-serpent.firstAction: Slithers toward a nearby storm drain grate, wrapping around a pipe and pulling it slightly askew to create a drag point.
monster:paperclipped-serpent.lore: Sorts chaos into order, violently. Drags loose ends into administrative limbo. Hates open windows and unfilleted paperwork. Often found coiled around radiator pipes.
monster:paperclipped-serpent.encounterText: It flows over the ground like liquid wire, shedding tiny slips of typed text that flutter to the floor. Its body twists into intricate knots, and it hums a low, bureaucratic tune.

monster:gutter-grub-swarm.name: Gutter Grub Swarm
monster:gutter-grub-swarm.description: A writhing mass of maggot-like creatures that feed on spilled soda and unravel shoelaces, slowing pursuit.

attacks.name: Sticky Swarm
attacks.flavor: Coats target in sugary resin, weighing down movements and making surfaces unnaturally tacky.

attacks.name: Lace Unravel
attacks.flavor: Trips target by simultaneously chewing through every shoe string, sending them sprawling onto the pavement.

specialAbilities: Carbonated Feast: Regenerates 1 HP each round while standing in liquid or consuming a consumable drink. Dissolves if doused in vinegar or citrus juice.

tactics.specialBehavior: Swarms, imposes disadvantage on movement, focuses on footwear/low gear. Used as path blockers and ambush triggers.

monster:gutter-grub-swarm.firstAction: Spills outward across the path, dissolving adhesive on boots and creating a sticky, slow-moving hazard.
monster:gutter-grub-swarm.lore: Scavengers of suburban runoff. Breed rapidly near leaking water fountains and picnic areas. Fear fire and strong citrus. Leave behind a sticky residue that attracts ants and bad luck.
monster:gutter-grub-swarm.encounterText: A glossy, syrupy river of chitin and slime oozes from a cracked drainpipe. Hundreds of pale, segmented bodies writhe together, glistening with cherry-red fluid and tangled laces. They part like a living carpet to let larger horrors pass.

monster:debt-collector-demon.name: Debt Collector Demon
monster:debt-collector-demon.description: A tall, clipboard-wielding fiend that marks expiration dates on living beings and collects late fees in breath.

attacks.name: Ledger Strike
attacks.flavor: Heavy book slams down with a resonant boom, inflicting a stacking penalty on future rolls.

attacks.name: Breath Tax
attacks.flavor: Siphons air from lungs, causing exhaustion-level fatigue and a chilling numbness in the extremities.

specialAbilities: Late Fee: Each round a marked target fails to pay a requested item/favor, they take 1d4 psychic damage and lose 1 point of maximum HP until the debt is cleared.

tactics.specialBehavior: Mark targets, applies penalties over time, uses psychological pressure. Prefers ranged/area effects and exploiting failed saves.

monster:debt-collector-demon.firstAction: Raises the clipboard, tapping a date. A visible timer appears on the nearest player, ticking down silently.
monster:debt-collector-demon.lore: Manages the subterranean economy. Tracks favors, time, and breath owed to the neighborhood. Never breaks a contract, but always finds a loophole. Drinks espresso that tastes like regret.
monster:debt-collector-demon.encounterText: It steps from behind a hedge trimmed into perfect rectangles. Impeccably dressed in a charcoal suit woven from shadow, it taps a silver pen against a leather-bound ledger. Wherever it points, a glowing red countdown appears above your head.

monster:static-phantom.name: Static Phantom
monster:static-phantom.description: A glitching television head that broadcasts distorted warnings and rewinds nearby actions three seconds into the past.

attacks.name: Signal Interference
attacks.flavor: Disrupts concentration and spell slots with a wave of electromagnetic fuzz that tastes like aluminum foil.

attacks.name: Rewind Pulse
attacks.flavor: Reverses a target's last action, forcing them to repeat it or suffer confusion and temporal nausea.

specialAbilities: Channel Surf: As a bonus action, swaps places with any creature currently within 10 feet, leaving behind a temporary afterimage that takes one weak attack next turn.

tactics.specialBehavior: Disrupts rhythm, rewinds player successes, uses area denial based on signal range. Targets combo chains and healing spells.

monster:static-phantom.firstAction: Screen flares bright, emitting a burst of static that makes sounds echo backward and visual trails lag behind movement.
monster:static-phantom.lore: Records mistakes and replays them. Haunts basements and dead channels. Rewinds local time in short bursts, often undoing successful hits or healing. Powered by forgotten cable subscriptions.
monster:static-phantom.encounterText: It floats at knee height, a bulky CRT monitor casing buzzing with white noise. The screen flickers between faces of townsfolk and rolling test patterns. When it speaks, the audio skips and loops.

monster:neighborly-shade.name: Neighborly Shade
monster:neighborly-shade.description: A faceless silhouette that mimics the posture of anyone standing nearby, then steps forward to claim their place.

attacks.name: Role Swap
attacks.flavor: Teleports positions with target, swapping equipment, status effects, and line-of-sight instantly.

attacks.name: Mimic Strike
attacks.flavor: Uses target's own fighting style against them, moving with uncanny familiarity and striking at expected blind spots.

specialAbilities: Polite Displacement: When attacked, can voluntarily step aside, causing the attack to hit the PC it was mirroring instead. Once per encounter.

tactics.specialBehavior: Mimicry, misdirection, targets key roles/support, exploits distraction. Uses positioning to swap allies with hazards or enemies.

monster:neighborly-shade.firstAction: Stands rigidly behind a PC, copying their weapon draw and stance exactly, waiting for their guard to drop.
monster:neighborly-shade.lore: Replaces people who stop paying attention to themselves. Takes over routines, jobs, and relationships. Only fully materializes when someone else looks away. Smells of lavender detergent and silence.
monster:neighborly-shade.encounterText: It stands just beyond the porch light, a flat cutout of absolute darkness shaped exactly like the person it's watching. It holds a mug, leans on a fence, or adjusts an imaginary collar—perfectly mirroring your stance. Then it steps out of the silhouette's outline and walks toward you.

monster:the-adjuster.name: The Adjuster
monster:the-adjuster.description: A colossal, multi-limbed auditor made of stacked ledgers and brass scales, capable of rewriting local physics through redaction.

attacks.name: Redaction Wave
attacks.flavor: Erases a chosen mechanic/effect for 1 round, accompanied by the sound of tearing paper and a sudden vacuum of meaning.

attacks.name: Ledger Crush
attacks.flavor: Slams stacked books down with tremendous force, crushing terrain and sending shards of glued vellum flying like shrapnel.

specialAbilities: Final Audit: At 50% HP, begins counting down from 10. Each round, it removes one random party resource/item/ability permanently until the count reaches zero or is interrupted by presenting a signed waiver (item:detention-slip-of-binding filled out correctly).

tactics.specialBehavior: Area control, modifies environment/rules, prioritizes breaking party cohesion, uses redaction to remove abilities/items temporarily. Shifts gravity and terrain layout.

monster:the-adjuster.firstAction: Lowers a giant brass stamp, pressing it into the floor. A zone appears where gravity shifts 90 degrees and furniture floats upward.
monster:the-adjuster.lore: Oversees the grand accounting of Yellow Hollow. Decides what stays, what goes, and what gets erased from the record. Speaks in audit reports and fine print. Cannot be killed, only audited out of existence by proving equitable exchange.
monster:the-adjuster.encounterText: The ceiling of the municipal chamber peels back like drywall. What descends is a towering edifice of parchment, iron brackets, and weeping ink. Six articulated arms manipulate giant calipers and stamp seals that thud like heartbeats. Its core is a massive balance scale, tipping slowly between gold coins and human teeth.

encounter:the-streetlight-standoff.name: The Streetlight Standoff
encounter:the-streetlight-standoff.description: A sentient, bureaucratic lamppost blocks the cul-de-sac entrance, demanding a "shared emotional equity" payment before illuminating the path to the guttermouth. The light pulses with a sickly yellow hue, and its shade rattles like teeth when approached.
encounter:the-streetlight-standoff.text: The air smells of ozone and burnt sugar. Directly ahead, a cast-iron lamppost leans aggressively over the sidewalk. Its bulb doesn't just glow; it throbs, expanding and contracting like a dilated pupil. A voice, sounding like a radio tuned between stations, crackles from the filament. "Passage requires recency. Joint asset verification. State your contribution."
encounter:the-streetlight-standoff.gmGuidance: Run this as a social negotiation where players must offer a memory they share. The lamppost cares about the *value* of the memory, not the content. If the party splits up or has conflicting memories, the light dims and gets colder. Penny Wisp can help translate the lamp's dialect of suburban metaphors.
outcomes: The party shares a genuine memory; the light flares warm white, illuminating a safe path, but the memory fades from their minds forever.
The party offers a transactional memory or lies; the light accepts but casts long, grasping shadows that impose disadvantage on stealth until morning.
The party fails to provide value; the light goes dark, and the temperature drops sharply, forcing them to backtrack or burn an item to generate heat.

encounter:candy-cane-ambush.name: Candy Cane Ambush
encounter:candy-cane-ambush.description: Crystallized confectionery spikes rain from the overhanging branches of a massive, twisted oak tree. The Cul-de-Sac Cyclops lurks in the trunk, hurling peppermint shrapnel, while Hoarding Imps scurry through the leaves to steal dropped gear.
encounter:candy-cane-ambush.text: Suddenly, the sky fills with the sound of crunching glass. Red-and-white striped shards plummet from the canopy, embedding themselves in the lawn with wet thuds. Through the falling candy, a massive, single eye blinks open in the bark of the oak. The Cyclops roars—a sound like tearing foil—and hurls a volley of razor-sharp peppermints. Below, small figures in stained cardigans dive for any equipment left unguarded.
encounter:candy-cane-ambush.gmGuidance: The terrain is hazardous due to the raining spikes. Players can take cover behind cars or storm drains. The Cyclops is the primary threat; defeating it stops the rain. The Imps are minions that prioritize stealing items; if they grab something, they flee to the Cyclops for protection. Use cartoon physics for the spikes—they bounce harmlessly if caught but pierce armor on impact.
outcomes: The Cyclops is defeated; the tree sighs and sheds its remaining candy, revealing a hidden access hatch to the drains.
The party escapes to the gutter; the Imps successfully loot a significant item and retreat with it.
The party bargains with the Cyclops by offering a rare sweet; it spares them but marks them with a sticky residue that attracts swarms later.

encounter:the-hoa-meeting.name: The HOA Meeting
encounter:the-hoa-meeting.description: The party stumbles upon a gathering of suburban ghosts and spirits in a spectral recreation of a community center. Reginald Quill presides, enforcing arbitrary property laws while subtly testing whether the newcomers belong in the neighborhood hierarchy.
encounter:the-hoa-meeting.text: Fluorescent lights hum with a low, headache-inducing drone. Rows of folding chairs are occupied by translucent figures wearing beige slacks and floral dresses. At the front, Reginald Quill taps a gavel made of fossilized bone against a lectern. "Item four: Unregistered souls loitering in Zone 7. New arrivals detected. Please state your zoning classification and reason for existing within these lot lines."
encounter:the-hoa-meeting.gmGuidance: Quill is less interested in justice than in procedure. He will demand paperwork, fees, or favors. Mrs. Gable is a nervous neighbor who might slip information if she thinks the party isn't going to report her for growing pumpkins out of season. Success involves navigating bureaucracy rather than combat; failure results in fines paid in years of lifespan or magical liens placed on the party's belongings.
outcomes: The party produces valid (or convincingly forged) papers; Quill stamps their foreheads with a 'Good Neighbor' seal, granting advantage on future social checks with spirits.
The party argues effectively; Quill relents but assigns them a probationary task to prove their worth.
The party fails the inspection; Mrs. Gable points out a violation, and the party is levied a debt of three days of silence, during which they cannot speak or cast verbal spells.

encounter:drainpipe-descent.name: Drainpipe Descent
encounter:drainpipe-descent.description: A narrow, corrugated metal chute spirals down into the earth. The pipe is slick with grease and grime, and rhythmic surges of water threaten to sweep unwary climbers away. Trust and timing are essential to navigate the drop without losing grip or sanity.
encounter:drainpipe-descent.text: The storm grate groans as it's pried open, revealing a throat of rusted iron. The air rising from below carries the scent of old pennies and forgotten toys. Water drips from the ceiling in perfect, maddening intervals—*plink, plink, plink*—matching the beat of a distant, distorted nursery rhyme. The walls curve inward, and the darkness below seems to breathe.
encounter:drainpipe-descent.gmGuidance: This is a cooperative challenge. Players must synchronize their movements with the dripping water or risk slipping. Physical attributes matter, but so does communication. One player can anchor others, creating mechanical bonuses for teamwork. Failure means sliding further down, potentially losing items or arriving separated.
outcomes: All players descend safely and reach the Guttermouth Drain ready to proceed.
One player slips and falls an extra thirty feet, taking minor damage but landing near a cache of discarded toys that contain useful components.
The party descends but loses cohesion; they arrive separated by distance, requiring them to find each other in the dark before proceeding.

choices.text: Anchoring Teamwork: Two players form a human chain, using ropes or limbs to secure the third.
choices.successOutcome: The chain holds firm; the third player descends quickly and safely, gaining a +1 bonus to the next check.
choices.failureOutcome: The rope frays or a hand slips; the player takes a fall but catches a protruding bolt, revealing a hidden compartment with a spare carabiner.

choices.text: Rhythm Timing: Wait for the exact moment the water surge pauses to make a risky leap to a lower ledge.
choices.successOutcome: You time the jump perfectly, landing silently and finding a dry spot to rest briefly.
choices.failureOutcome: You mistime the leap; you land hard, taking damage, but the impact dislodges a jar of preserved pickled eyes that roll down to aid vision.

choices.text: Bold Climbing: Scale the vertical section using sheer strength and grit, ignoring the water hazards.
choices.successOutcome: You haul yourself up rapidly, bypassing the slippery sections entirely.
choices.failureOutcome: Your grip gives way; you slide back down ten feet, bruising your ego, but your struggle causes a loose patch of rust to fall, exposing a ventilation shaft leading sideways.

encounter:paperclip-puzzle.name: Paperclip Puzzle
encounter:paperclip-puzzle.description: A massive wall of interlocking silver loops blocks the corridor. The Paperclipped Serpent coils around the mechanism, watching with multifaceted eyes. Solving the puzzle requires understanding tension, sequence, and the metaphorical weight of connection.
encounter:paperclip-puzzle.text: The metal gleams under the flickering fluorescent tubes overhead. Hundreds of paperclips, each the size of a dinner fork, weave together in an impossibly complex lattice. Some loops are taut, pulling against others; some hang loose, dangling like tongues. The serpent shifts, its scales clicking softly. "To pass, you must untie what binds without breaking the thread," it whispers, its voice dry as onion skin.
encounter:paperclip-puzzle.gmGuidance: The puzzle is abstract. Players must manipulate the clips based on clues found earlier (e.g., a rhyme about 'holding on' vs 'letting go'). There is no single mathematical solution; it relies on lateral thinking. The serpent will offer hints if asked politely, but it enjoys watching people struggle.
outcomes: The wall dissolves into a pile of loose clips, opening the path.
The serpent is impressed and allows passage, but demands a small favor later.
The wall reforms tighter, trapping a limb or item, forcing a retry with new insight.

choices.text: Pull the Golden Loop: The clip with a slight bend seems to be the keystone.
choices.successOutcome: It slides free with a satisfying click; the tension releases, and the wall parts.
choices.failureOutcome: It's a decoy; the wall snaps shut, pinching the player's arm for minor damage, but reveals a loose clip hidden behind the frame.

choices.text: Whisper a Secret to the Serpent: The serpent values secrets over logic.
choices.successOutcome: The serpent nods approvingly and nudges the correct clip loose with its nose.
choices.failureOutcome: The serpent finds the secret boring; it hisses and knocks a heavy clip onto the player's foot, causing pain but showing the path of least resistance.

choices.text: Force the Knot: Apply brute force to snap the central knot.
choices.successOutcome: The metal bends; the knot yields, and the wall collapses.
choices.failureOutcome: The metal springs back; the player is thrown backward, dazed, but the violent motion shakes a key loose from the serpent's collar.

encounter:tupperware-tangle.name: Tupperware Tangle
encounter:tupperware-tangle.description: A swarm of plastic food containers buzzes through the air like angry hornets. They seek to seal exits and trap intruders. The Tupperware Ghoul directs the swarm from atop a stack of mismatched lids, clacking its mandibles impatiently.
encounter:tupperware-tangle.text: The air fills with the high-pitched whine of spinning plastic. Containers slam into surfaces, locking instantly with audible clicks. A large, grotesque figure made of fused Tupperware pieces and dried lasagna scuttles across the ceiling. "Fresh stock!" it shrieks. "Seal! Store! Preserve!" The swarm dives, aiming to box the party in.
encounter:tupperware-tangle.gmGuidance: The Ghoul is the leader; destroying it causes the swarm to become disorganized and easier to handle. The containers create difficult terrain and can entangle players. Cartoon physics apply: containers bounce off heads, and popping a lid releases a puff of stale air that might obscure vision. Players can use fire or sharp objects to burst the plastic.
outcomes: The Ghoul is popped; the swarm disperses, leaving behind a pristine container that can preserve food indefinitely.
The party is sealed in a giant container; they must escape before running out of air or being moved to storage.
The party negotiates with the Ghoul by promising to return a missing lid; it grants safe passage.

encounter:bargain-at-the-crossroads.name: Bargain at the Crossroads
encounter:bargain-at-the-crossroads.description: A demon stands at a T-intersection formed by shifting asphalt. Broker Vane offers a shortcut through the underworld in exchange for a binding promise. The contract is written in microscopic font, and the devil is always in the details.
encounter:bargain-at-the-crossroads.text: The road surface ripples like liquid tar. In the center of the junction stands Broker Vane, adjusting his tie which is knotted with a strip of human hair. He holds out a scroll that unrolls endlessly into the darkness. "Time is money, and money is time," he purrs. "Sign here, and I'll cut your travel time by half. Just a small clause regarding your firstborn's shadow, nothing more."
encounter:bargain-at-the-crossroads.gmGuidance: Vane is charming but predatory. Players can read the fine print (WIT check) to find loopholes, negotiate terms, or accept the deal. The 'firstborn's shadow' is a classic trope, but the actual cost might be subtler, like 'the color of your laughter' or 'memory of home'. Success saves time but may incur a hidden cost later.
outcomes: The party signs; they gain a teleportation token but inherit a subtle curse related to the signed clause.
The party finds a loophole; Vane grumbles and lets them pass, though he notes he'll be watching.
The party refuses; Vane snaps his fingers, and the path twists, adding a detour through a dangerous zone.

encounter:static-maze.name: Static Maze
encounter:static-maze.description: The corridors of the television studio shift and distort like a bad signal. Walls phase in and out of existence, and the Static Phantom drifts through the glitching geometry. Players must tune their movement to stable frequencies to navigate without getting lost.
encounter:static-maze.text: The hallway stretches and compresses, accompanied by the hiss of white noise. Colors bleed into each other, and shapes resolve into familiar objects—a toaster, a cat, a screaming face—before dissolving again. A figure made of snowflakes glides past, leaving a trail of dead pixels. Every step feels like walking through a dream you're trying to wake up from.
encounter:static-maze.gmGuidance: This is an environmental puzzle. Players must identify stable patterns in the chaos. Using items that emit sound or light can help stabilize local reality. The Phantom can disrupt their focus, causing them to misstep. Failure results in moving in the wrong direction or becoming trapped in a loop.
outcomes: The party tunes to the right frequency and emerges in the control room.
The Phantom merges with the party temporarily, granting temporary invisibility but causing hallucinations.
The party gets stuck in a loop; they must burn an item to reset the signal, losing progress.

choices.text: Hum the Theme Song: Match your voice to the background hum to stabilize the local area.
choices.successOutcome: The walls lock into place, revealing a clear path forward.
choices.failureOutcome: Your voice cracks; the static intensifies, and the Phantom mocks you, dealing psychic damage but revealing a hidden switch.

choices.text: Flash a Mirror: Reflect the chaotic light to create a moment of clarity.
choices.successOutcome: The glare freezes the maze for a few seconds, allowing a quick dash.
choices.failureOutcome: The mirror shatters; shards embed in your hand, causing pain, but the reflection shows the correct route.

choices.text: Dance the Glitch: Move erratically to blend in with the shifting geometry.
choices.successOutcome: You slip through the gaps unnoticed by the Phantom.
choices.failureOutcome: You move too much; the maze grabs you and spins you around, depositing you ten feet back, but you land on a button that opens a side door.

encounter:debt-collection-raid.name: Debt Collection Raid
encounter:debt-collection-raid.description: Enforcers burst forth from the shadows, led by a Debt Collector Demon. They aren't here to kill; they're here to seize collateral. The party's belongings, memories, or even body parts are fair game to satisfy outstanding balances.
encounter:debt-collection-raid.text: "Audit complete!" barks the Demon, holding a clipboard that bleeds ink. "You're short on three counts: unpaid wonder, overdue courage, and a missing sock from 1998. Payment due now." Two Asphalt Wraiths materialize, their bodies composed of blacktop and broken curbs. They lunge, not to strike, but to grab and bind.
encounter:debt-collection-raid.gmGuidance: The demons prefer non-lethal methods initially; they want assets. Players can fight, but damaging the collectors might increase the debt. Alternatively, players can offer items or promises to settle the debt. The Asphalt Wraiths are tough and resistant to slashing damage. Winning the fight might mean surviving the raid, but the debt remains unless settled.
outcomes: The party pays the debt with items; the demons leave satisfied, marking the party as current.
The party fights off the demons; the debt is forgiven, but the demons vow to collect later with interest.
The party loses a significant item or memory; the debt is cleared, but the loss has lasting effects.

encounter:mirror-maze-negotiation.name: Mirror Maze Negotiation
encounter:mirror-maze-negotiation.description: Dozens of mirrors line the walls, each reflecting a distorted version of the party. The reflections step out of their frames, arguing over who owes whom. The Neighborly Shade orchestrates the dispute, waiting for the party to settle the accounts before letting them reclaim their own images.
encounter:mirror-maze-negotiation.text: Every surface is glass, and every reflection moves independently. One copy of you is laughing; another is crying. They bicker loudly. "He took my umbrella!" "No, you stole my confidence!" The Neighborly Shade floats among them, a tall figure in a suit of cracked glass. "Settle the ledger," it rasps. "Or remain here as decorative duplicates forever."
outcomes: The disputes are settled; the reflections return to the mirrors, and the party gains a mirror shard that can reveal hidden truths.
The party compromises poorly; the reflections merge with the party, granting random abilities but confusing their identity.
The party breaks a mirror; the Shade attacks, but the party can escape through the shattered frame.

choices.text: Listen to the Arguments: Pay close attention to hear the underlying truth.
choices.successOutcome: You identify the core grievance and propose a fair solution; the reflections bow and return.
choices.failureOutcome: You miss a key detail; your proposal angers the Shade, who steals a piece of your shadow.

choices.text: Break the Central Mirror: Shatter the nexus to release the reflections forcefully.
choices.successOutcome: The glass explodes; the reflections scatter harmlessly, and the path clears.
choices.failureOutcome: The mirror holds; the shockwave knocks you back, and the Shade uses the distraction to swap places with you.

choices.text: Offer a Trade: Propose exchanging a personal item for the reflections.
choices.successOutcome: The Shade accepts the trade; the reflections return, and you keep your item intact.
choices.failureOutcome: The Shade devalues your item; you lose the item and still have to solve the puzzle.

encounter:the-final-count.name: The Final Count
encounter:the-final-count.description: A rapid-fire sequence of audits, dodges, and last-second signings as the underworld's closing bell approaches. The Adjuster oversees the process, ticking away the seconds. The party must balance saving the child, escaping, and managing their accumulated debts in a blur of activity.
encounter:the-final-count.text: The ground trembles as a great bell tolls in the distance. Papers fly through the air like confetti. The Adjuster appears, a towering figure of ledgers and calipers. "Time's up! Sign here! Run there! Don't look back!" The environment becomes a gauntlet of deadlines. Every second counts, and mistakes compound instantly.
encounter:the-final-count.gmGuidance: Run this as a series of quick challenges. Players choose actions each round, and the Adjuster imposes consequences. The goal is to reach the exit or rescue the child before the bell finishes ringing. Failure means partial success or acquiring a major debt. Use a clock or timer mechanic to heighten tension.
outcomes: The party escapes with the child and minimal debt; they emerge victorious but changed.
The party escapes but leaves behind a loved one or item; the debt is substantial.
The party is caught; they survive but are bound to Yellow Hollow as permanent residents.

choices.text: Sign the Waiver: Accept whatever terms the Adjuster dictates to speed up the process.
choices.successOutcome: You sign rapidly; the Adjuster stamps your forehead, and you gain a burst of speed to the exit.
choices.failureOutcome: You sign the wrong line; you gain speed but owe a favor to the Adjuster, callable at any time.

choices.text: Grab the Child: Dash to rescue the target despite the chaos.
choices.successOutcome: You snatch the child and dodge the Adjuster's grasp, reaching safety.
choices.failureOutcome: You grab the child but trip; the Adjuster pins you down, delaying escape by a critical amount.

choices.text: Defy the Audit: Refuse to play by the rules and fight the system.
choices.successOutcome: You tear up the ledger; the Adjuster falters, allowing a window of opportunity.
choices.failureOutcome: The ledger absorbs your defiance; you are marked as a rebel, and the debt doubles.

encounter:hearthstone-reunion.name: Hearthstone Reunion
encounter:hearthstone-reunion.description: In the quiet heart of Hearthstone Hollow, the Pendelton family gathers around a glowing stone. Separated by the journey's trials, they reconcile their differences, unlocking latent cooperative abilities fueled by the magic of family loyalty.
encounter:hearthstone-reunion.text: The stone radiates a warmth that feels like a hug. Arthur sits with his head in his hands; Beatrice tends to a wound with practiced calm. Milo and Lily sit close, whispering. As the family comes together, the stone pulses brighter, weaving their individual energies into a shared tapestry. For a moment, the horrors of the underworld feel distant, held back by the sheer force of their bond.
encounter:hearthstone-reunion.gmGuidance: This is a roleplay-heavy encounter. Players describe how their characters interact and reconcile. Successful roleplay grants bonuses to the party, such as shared HP pools, combined attacks, or immunity to fear. The GM should encourage emotional beats and references to earlier events. This encounter can restore resources or heal conditions.
outcomes: The family bonds deeply; they gain a 'Family Shield' ability that lasts until the next major battle.
Reconciliation is partial; they gain a minor boost but harbor unresolved tensions.
A secret is revealed during the reunion; it changes the understanding of one character's motivation.

location:cul-de-sac-of-echoing-lawns.name: Cul-de-Sac of Echoing Lawns
location:cul-de-sac-of-echoing-lawns.description: A perfectly symmetrical surface street where wind chimes hum off-key and shadows stretch longer than the sun allows. The asphalt is too black, the grass too green, creating a visual dissonance that makes the eyes water.
location:cul-de-sac-of-echoing-lawns.atmosphere: The air smells like fresh cut grass and ozone. A low, constant thrumming vibration rises from beneath the pavement, felt in the teeth rather than heard.
features: Climb the telephone pole to see over the manicured hedges for a better vantage point of the neighborhood.
Bargain with the wind chimes by offering a shiny object to hear the true direction of the draft coming from the drains.
Investigate the shadows under the porch lights; they move independently of the light sources when no one is looking.
Kick the storm drain grate to test its stability before descending.
Hazard: Disorientation: The geometry of the street subtly shifts if you stare at the horizon too long.

connections.travel: Descend through the grate near the center of the cul-de-sac; the drop is slippery with condensation.
connections.observation: The wind chimes above grow louder as you approach the drain, singing a discordant melody.
connections.risk: Sudden gusts of warm, sulfurous air can knock you off balance before you reach the ladder.

connections.travel: Enter the front door of the Pendelton residence at the top of the cul-de-sac; the walk feels shorter than it should.
connections.observation: The family dog refuses to cross the threshold back into the house, whining at the dark.
connections.risk: None, unless you are being watched by the neighbors' windows.

secrets: The "children" playing hopscotch on the driveway are not children, but echoes of previous victims who haven't been taken yet.

location:the-guttermouth-drain.name: The Guttermouth Drain
location:the-guttermouth-drain.description: A rusted storm grate that exhales damp, sulfur-tinged air, serving as the primary descent route into the subterranean grid. Below lies a vertical shaft lined with roots and ancient brick, dripping with slime.
location:the-guttermouth-drain.atmosphere: Damp, cold, and smelling of wet iron and rotting vegetation. The sound of rushing water echoes up from below, sounding almost like voices.
features: Listen closely to the rushing water to decipher the muffled screams or bargains of those who came before.
Scrape the slime off the walls to reveal hidden graffiti left by previous adventurers.
Use the ladder rungs to hang upside down, gaining a different perspective on the shaft's depth.
Collect the dripping water in a container; it tastes sweet but causes temporary hallucinations.
Hazard: Darkness: Without light, movement speed is halved and stealth checks have disadvantage.

connections.travel: Climb the rusted ladder back up to the surface world; the climb is tiring due to the slick moss.
connections.observation: The sky looks impossibly small and bright from down here.
connections.risk: Slippery surfaces; falling means taking damage and losing gear.

connections.travel: Follow the main sewer pipe downstream; the tunnel narrows and the walls become sticky.
connections.observation: The water changes from clear to a viscous, pinkish hue.
connections.risk: Sticky tar pits begin shortly after the pipe ends.

connections.travel: Take the left fork at the junction marked with a faded HOA logo; the tunnel widens into a vaulted chamber.
connections.observation: Paper scraps float in the stagnant water, forming shapes of faces.
connections.risk: Encounter debt collectors patrolling the perimeter.

secrets: The grate was installed not to keep things out, but to keep something specific IN.

location:paperclip-palace.name: Paperclip Palace
location:paperclip-palace.description: A sprawling labyrinth of filing cabinets, pneumatic tubes, and fluorescent-lit waiting rooms where time dilates and forms stack endlessly. The architecture is a mix of brutalist concrete and mid-century office aesthetics.
location:paperclip-palace.atmosphere: The hum of fluorescent lights is deafening. The air is dry and recycled, tasting of toner dust. Shadows are sharp and geometric.
features: Search the filing cabinets for your own personal file; knowing your debts might help in negotiations.
Ride a pneumatic tube to travel quickly between wings, aiming for the target canister.
Fix the flickering fluorescent lights to reduce the stress penalty in the area.
Barter with the clerks for access to restricted archives using candy or memories.
Hazard: Bureaucratic Paralysis: If you fail a social check, you must fill out a form before acting again.

connections.travel: Exit through the service elevator doors; they open directly onto the muddy bank of the underground river.
connections.observation: The elevator buttons only have numbers that don't correspond to floors.
connections.risk: The elevator may malfunction, dropping you to a random floor.

connections.travel: Follow the pneumatic tube system; ride a canister to the broadcast wing.
connections.observation: The canisters rattle violently as they pass through areas with high static interference.
connections.risk: Getting crushed if the tube is blocked.

connections.travel: Find the spiral staircase labeled "Archives"; it leads to the basement level of the family home.
connections.observation: The stairs seem to go on forever, counting up indefinitely.
connections.risk: Time dilation: You may spend hours climbing what feels like minutes.

secrets: Your file contains a lie about why you were chosen; correcting it weakens the demons' hold on you.

location:candy-coated-canyon.name: Candy-Coated Canyon
location:candy-coated-canyon.description: A toxic ravine littered with crystallized sugar, shattered wrappers, and sticky tar pits that trap unwary footsteps. The ground is uneven, composed of hardened caramel and jagged chocolate rocks.
location:candy-coated-canyon.atmosphere: Sweet, cloying scent of burnt sugar mixed with decay. Bright colors that hurt the eyes. The heat is oppressive.
features: Break apart the crystal formations to harvest raw sugar for crafting or trade.
Test the integrity of the tar pits by throwing objects before crossing.
Hunt for stray candy monsters lurking in the wrapper piles.
Build a raft from discarded packaging to navigate the syrup rivers.
Hazard: Sugar Rush/Coma: Consuming too much local flora causes erratic behavior or unconsciousness.

connections.travel: Swim upstream against the sugary current to find the drain entrance.
connections.observation: Floating debris includes toys and lost shoes.
connections.risk: Sticky currents slow swimming speed significantly.

connections.travel: Climb the ridge of compressed trash bags to reach the studio's antenna array.
connections.observation: The TV signals interfere with the candy's sweetness, making it taste metallic.

connections.travel: Dig through the root cellar wall to emerge in the backyard garden.
connections.observation: The soil is rich and dark, contrasting with the bright canyon.
connections.risk: Roots try to grab your ankles.

secrets: The canyon is formed from the collective wishes of children who never grew up.

location:the-static-television-studio.name: The Static Television Studio
location:the-static-television-studio.description: A decaying broadcast facility where animated entities warp reality through channel surfing, frequency tuning, and signal interference. The set designs are surreal and incomplete, blending cartoon logic with live-action horror.
location:the-static-television-studio.atmosphere: Visual distortion, color bleeding, and sudden drops in volume. The smell of ozone and old plastic.
features: Tune the large broadcast monitors to find the frequency where Lily's voice is trapped.
Explore the green screen sets to find hidden passages behind the painted backgrounds.
Defeat the static ghosts by disrupting their signal with a strong electromagnetic pulse.
Watch the looping footage of the town's history to uncover clues about the underworld.
Hazard: Reality Warp: Random effects occur every round, changing gravity or sound.

connections.travel: Return via the pneumatic tube network to the administration wing.
connections.observation: The tubes glow with static electricity.

connections.travel: Jump from the antenna tower into the canyon below.
connections.observation: You land in a pile of soft, compressed marshmallows.
connections.risk: Broken bones if you miss the landing zone.

connections.travel: Turn on the main broadcast monitor; step through the screen into the family living room.
connections.observation: The transition is jarring, like waking from a dream.
connections.risk: The screen may shatter, trapping you inside temporarily.

secrets: The studio is broadcasting the family's fears in real-time to entertain the demons.

location:hearthstone-hollow.name: Hearthstone Hollow
location:hearthstone-hollow.description: The family's basement-turned-anchor point, lined with mismatched rugs and a single working fireplace that glows when loved ones draw near. It serves as the safe haven and starting point for descents.
location:hearthstone-hollow.atmosphere: Warm, inviting, smelling of woodsmoke and old books. The contrast to the hellish underworld is stark and comforting.
features: Rest by the fire to recover hit points and sanity; the warmth provides a bonus to morale.
Check the candy bowl to see if any treats remain or if new ones have appeared.
Examine the family photos on the mantle for hidden messages or clues.
Prepare gear and discuss strategy in the quiet comfort of home.
Hazard: Complacency: Staying too long may cause characters to forget their mission.

connections.travel: Go upstairs and out the front door to the suburban street.
connections.observation: The outside world seems brighter and sharper.
connections.risk: Neighbors may notice you leaving at odd hours.

connections.travel: Take the spiral staircase up to the archives.
connections.observation: The air gets drier and quieter.

connections.travel: Step through the television screen in the living room.

secrets: The fireplace is connected to the heart of the underworld; feeding it the right item can open a shortcut.

npc:arthur-pendelton.name: Arthur Pendelton
npc:arthur-pendelton.description: A man in a cardigan that has seen better decades, wearing reading glasses on a chain. He holds a clipboard like a shield and carries a multi-tool with the reverence of a holy relic.
npc:arthur-pendelton.personality: He treats supernatural horrors as logistical inconveniences. He speaks in passive voice to avoid blame and checks his watch when demons monologue. He is fiercely protective but expresses it through efficiency.

motivation.want: Restore order to the household ledger so Lily can be found without disrupting the mortgage payment schedule.
motivation.fear: Being perceived as incompetent or losing control of the family narrative.
motivation.lie: If he just follows the rules and fills out the right forms, the universe will behave logically.

dialogueHints: "Before we scream, let's establish a chain of command. I'll take point on the heavy lifting; Milo, you handle the sarcasm; Beatrice, keep the duct tape ready."
"This demon owes us three days of emotional labor. I expect restitution by Tuesday."

relationships.npc:beatrice-pendelton: Married partners who bicker over strategy but never over loyalty; she manages the chaos he tries to organize.
relationships.npc:milo-pendelton: Son who finds Arthur’s seriousness exhausting but secretly admires his stubbornness.
relationships.npc:lily-pendelton: Father who promised to always fix her broken toys, now trying to fix the unfixable underworld.
relationships.npc:reginald-quill: Professional rivalry; Arthur sees Quill as a bureaucratic obstacle, Quill sees Arthur as a delinquent account.

secrets: Arthur secretly believes he failed Lily by working too much, which is why he’s risking everything to get her back.

npc:beatrice-pendelton.name: Beatrice Pendelton
npc:beatrice-pendelton.description: A woman whose eyes miss nothing, dressed in practical layers. Her pockets bulge with an impossible assortment of items: spare keys, gum, a flashlight, and a half-eaten granola bar.
npc:beatrice-pendelton.personality: Resourceful, sharp-tongued, and terrifyingly prepared. She solves problems with duct tape and sheer willpower. She is the emotional anchor of the group, masking her fear with aggressive competence.

motivation.want: Ensure every member of the family returns home, even if they have to drag them back themselves.
motivation.fear: Losing her children to forces she cannot pack away in a box.
motivation.lie: If she keeps moving and fixing things, the hole in their lives won’t grow any wider.

dialogueHints: "I’ve got a backup plan for your backup plan. Now move."
"You think you’re scary? I raised two boys and survived HOA meetings. Try again."

relationships.npc:arthur-pendelton: Her counterbalance; where he calculates, she improvises.
relationships.npc:mrs-gable: Neighborly respect; Mrs. Gable knows Beatrice’s secrets, and Beatrice knows Mrs. Gable’s gossip network.
relationships.npc:lily-pendelton: Mother who packed Lily’s favorite stuffed bear in her pocket, hoping it acts as a beacon.

secrets: Beatrice has been hiding a medical diagnosis from the family, fearing it would make them worry more than the demons already do.

npc:milo-pendelton.name: Milo Pendelton
npc:milo-pendelton.description: A teenager with messy hair and a hoodie pulled up, constantly sketching in a notebook. He looks bored but his eyes are constantly scanning exits and threats.
npc:milo-pendelton.personality: Sardonic, observant, and deeply loyal beneath a layer of teenage apathy. He uses humor to deflect vulnerability and maps escape routes everywhere he goes.

motivation.want: Prove he’s capable and mature enough to be taken seriously, while surviving the weirdness.
motivation.fear: Being treated like a child by both his parents and the monsters.
motivation.lie: If he doesn’t care about anything, nothing can hurt him.

dialogueHints: "Great. A demon. Can we hurry? My phone is at 4% and my soul is at 2%."
"Look, if we die, I’m haunting this place first. At least the rent is reasonable."

relationships.npc:arthur-pendelton: Friction; Milo thinks Arthur is rigid, Arthur thinks Milo is reckless.
relationships.npc:penny-wisp: Unlikely ally; Penny respects Milo’s observational skills, and Milo trusts her strange warnings.
relationships.npc:lily-pendelton: Older brother who feels guilty for not protecting her, despite his protests.

secrets: Milo’s notebook isn’t just doodles; it contains accurate sketches of the underworld’s layout, drawn from vivid nightmares.

npc:lily-pendelton.name: Lily Pendelton
npc:lily-pendelton.description: A small girl in a tattered witch costume, glowing faintly with residual magic. She hums nursery rhymes backward, her eyes wide and slightly unfocused.
npc:lily-pendelton.personality: Ethereal, quiet, and strangely resilient. She exists partially in the ledger loop, aware of her family but unable to fully interact with the physical world yet.

motivation.want: To remember her name and find her way back to her family before she becomes just another entry in the book.
motivation.fear: Forgetting who she loves, becoming a number instead of a person.
motivation.lie: If she stays quiet and still, the accountants won’t notice her.

dialogueHints: (Humming backwards) "Ydaer... ydaer..."
"The man in the suit says I’m overdue. But I didn’t borrow anything."

relationships.npc:arthur-pendelton: Daughter who looks to her father for safety, unaware of the danger he faces for her.
relationships.npc:reginald-quill: Captive; Quill views her as a valuable asset due to her unique magical resonance.
relationships.npc:penny-wisp: Fellow spirit; Penny watches over Lily, acting as a temporary guardian.

secrets: Lily was taken because her laughter has a specific frequency that can unlock the deepest vaults of the underworld.

npc:reginald-quill.name: Reginald Quill
npc:reginald-quill.description: Tall and gaunt, wearing a pinstripe suit woven from yellowed tax forms. His face is smooth, almost featureless, except for a mouth that moves like a stamp hitting paper.
npc:reginald-quill.personality: Precise, polite, and utterly merciless. He speaks in receipts and measured threats. He believes emotion is a variable that complicates the equation.

motivation.want: Balance the books of the underworld by collecting all debts, including the Pendelton family’s hidden obligations.
motivation.fear: Chaos and unpredictability, which threaten the stability of the cosmic ledger.
motivation.lie: Everything has a price, and everyone pays eventually.

dialogueHints: "Your daughter’s presence is an outstanding liability. We propose a restructuring plan."
"Politeness is merely the grease that allows the gears of debt to turn smoothly."

relationships.npc:broker-vane: Employer and employee; Vane collects the initial debts, Quill finalizes the terms.
relationships.npc:silas-thorne: Rivalry; Thorne owes spiritual back taxes to Quill’s domain, creating tension between suburban development and underworld law.
relationships.npc:lily-pendelton: Collector and asset; Quill intends to keep Lily until the debt is paid.

secrets: Quill himself is not entirely alive; he is a construct of accumulated obligations, sustained only by the debts he collects.

npc:mrs-gable.name: Mrs. Gable
npc:mrs-gable.description: An elderly woman with lace curtains perpetually fluttering behind her window. She wears floral dresses and carries a basket of cookies that might be normal, or might be cursed.
npc:mrs-gable.personality: Gossipy, observant, and surprisingly knowledgeable. She knows everyone’s business and hoards secrets like contraband. She offers help in exchange for interesting stories.

motivation.want: Know what’s happening in Yellow Hollow before anyone else, maintaining her status as the town’s information hub.
motivation.fear: Becoming irrelevant or being left out of the main event.
motivation.lie: Information is power, and power keeps you safe.

dialogueHints: "Oh, dear. Did you hear? The Pendletons’ lawn gnomes blinked last night. Shiver me timbers."
"I know where the old property deeds are buried. For the right price — or the right story."

relationships.npc:beatrice-pendelton: Neighborly dynamic; Beatrice tolerates Mrs. Gable’s nosiness because it’s useful.
relationships.npc:silas-thorne: Informant; Mrs. Gable knows Silas’s past deals and uses this knowledge to leverage favors.
relationships.npc:penny-wisp: Observer; Mrs. Gable saw Penny appear and disappear, adding to her collection of neighborhood mysteries.

secrets: Mrs. Gable is not just a neighbor; she is the last descendant of the family that originally sold the land to the underworld entities.

npc:silas-thorne.name: Silas Thorne
npc:silas-thorne.description: A slick man in an expensive suit, smiling with too much confidence. He carries a tablet that displays blueprints of the underworld overlaid with property lines.
npc:silas-thorne.personality: Ambitious, charming, and opportunistic. He paved over ancient ley lines with vinyl siding and now owes spiritual back taxes. He sees monsters as potential tenants.

motivation.want: Expand his real estate empire into the underworld, turning Hell into a high-end residential district.
motivation.fear: Going bankrupt, both financially and spiritually.
motivation.lie: Progress justifies any means, especially if you pave over the evidence.

dialogueHints: "Imagine the views from the penthouse suite, right above the Lake of Fire. Prime real estate."
